scope: ELF versus qmd live memory-evolution behavior, current-vs-historical conflict diagnosis, and optimization directions

elf.diagnosis: ELF retrieved all required evidence but failed supersession jobs because conflict detection and lifecycle-aware current-vs-historical answer behavior were not emitted.

qmd.diagnosis: qmd had the same missing conflict-detection pattern and additionally missed three required evidence links, including the delete tombstone.

job_diagnosis.diagnosis: ELF retrieved current verdict, caveat, and rationale but did not cite the old not-ready verdict as historical; qmd also missed private-corpus caveat evidence.
Both retrieved the current runbook and supersession rationale but did not preserve the old quickstart path as historical conflict evidence.
Both answered the current done state and rationale but did not surface the earlier blocked state as superseded history.
ELF retrieved current preference and rationale but did not preserve the old terse preference as historical; qmd only returned rationale evidence.
Both retrieved current and historical owners but did not emit scored temporal-validity explanation or update rationale.
ELF retrieved tombstone and current plan evidence; qmd retrieved only the current plan and missed the tombstone.

elf_failure_pattern.interpretation: The issue is lifecycle-aware reconciliation and narration, not basic evidence retrieval.

not_allowed: ELF broadly beats qmd as a memory system
ELF has solved temporal memory evolution
Graphiti/Zep, mem0/OpenMemory, or Letta are beaten

optimization_directions.description: Detect current and historical evidence for the same claim, choose the current winner, preserve the historical loser, and cite update rationale.
Expose add/update/delete/ignore history and version links for user preference and entity memory changes.
Treat deletion and TTL tombstones as answerable evidence instead of only suppressing stale retrieval.
Hydrate trace artifacts with conflict candidates, current winners, historical losers, dropped candidates, and replay commands.

borrow_from.borrow: temporal fact windows, invalidation, supersession, and graph fact provenance
borrow_from.benchmark_gate: Graphiti/Zep temporal graph adapter for current, historical, and future-valid facts

borrow_from.borrow: entity-scoped history, lifecycle inspection, and memory UI/readback
borrow_from.benchmark_gate: entity and preference history readback with correction and deletion evidence

borrow_from.borrow: core memory blocks versus archival memory
borrow_from.benchmark_gate: core-vs-archival jobs for operating context and historical retrieval

borrow_from.borrow: local replay and candidate inspection ergonomics
borrow_from.benchmark_gate: ELF trace hydration with conflict candidates and replay commands
